items.title: RT by @WhiteHouse: Yesterday, I hosted 'Stump the Secretary' at @Freedom250's Great American State Fair.   Kids asked questions ranging from how fireworks are different colors to when we'll see nuclear reactors on the moon.   Absolutely loved the curiosity and creativity—the future is bright!
items.summary:
Yesterday, I hosted 'Stump the Secretary' at @Freedom250's Great American State Fair.
 
Kids asked questions ranging from how fireworks are different colors to when we'll see nuclear reactors on the moon.
 
Absolutely loved the curiosity and creativity—the future is bright!

Security Council LIVE: Amid rising regional tensions, members address Iran nuclear deal
items.summary: As tensions mount in the Middle East amid new strikes across the region, the Security Council is convening on Friday to discuss the status of its 2015 resolution on the Iran nuclear deal, the Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action (JCPOA). Stay tuned for live updates.

Ukraine war : Kyiv under Russian missile attack , officials say
What if Berlin needs its bunkers back?
items.summary: After the cold war, public air-raid shelters were turned into nightclubs and galleries. Now anxiety over conflict is growing again

items.title: Eleven people, including a child, have been injured in Kyiv during last night's Russian attack. Civilian infrastructure was hit even before the air raid alert was issued. Emergency crews are working at the sites of the strikes – apartment buildings, offices, and a theological seminary were damaged in the capital. Recovery efforts are also ongoing in the Odesa, Sumy, Kharkiv, and Chernihiv regions. Over the course of the night, Russia launched more than 120 drones and 12 missiles, half of them ballistic. Our defenders managed to shoot down most of the targets – but not the ballistic ones. We expect our partners to deliver on their promises regarding support packages agreed at the NATO Summit to help protect our people. We must move as swiftly as possible on licensing agreements for Patriots and the joint European anti-ballistic defense project. Currently, the top priority for all our institutions is to make this happen.
items.summary: Eleven people, including a child, have been injured in Kyiv during last night's Russian attack. Civilian infrastructure was hit even before the air raid alert was issued.  Emergency crews are working at the sites of the strikes – apartment buildings, offices, and a theological seminary were damaged in the capital. Recovery efforts are also ongoing in the Odesa, Sumy, Kharkiv, and Chernihiv regions

items.title: China Tells Refiners to Keep Fuel Output High as Iran War Drags
items.summary: China instructed some major refiners to keep fuel production high, according to people familiar with the matter, in an effort to protect domestic consumers as strikes in the Persian Gulf once again threaten oil shipments.
